pr.title: Patch transitive `qs` dependency to 6.15.2 to remediate Dependabot DoS alert
pr.body:
This PR addresses the open Dependabot alert for `qs` (remotely triggerable DoS in `stringify` under comma-format + `encodeValuesOnly`). The repository did not declare `qs` directly, so remediation is applied at the lockfile level for the transitive path in use.

- **Dependency remediation (lockfile-only)**
  - Updated `qs` from `6.15.0` to `6.15.2` in `pnpm-lock.yaml`.
  - The resolved transitive path is:
    - `vite-plugin-node-polyfills` → `node-stdlib-browser` → `url@0.11.4` → `qs`.

- **Blast-radius-oriented scope**
  - No application source changes.
  - No dependency graph expansion; only the vulnerable transitive version was bumped to the fixed release.

```yaml
# pnpm-lock.yaml (effective dependency edge)
url@0.11.4:
  dependencies:
    punycode: 1.4.1
    qs: 6.15.2
```

> [!WARNING]
>
> <details>
> <summary>Firewall rules blocked me from connecting to one or more addresses (expand for details)</summary>
>
> #### I tried to connect to the following addresses, but was blocked by firewall rules:
>
> - `https://api.github.com/repos/Renumics/spotlight/dependabot/alerts`
>   - Triggering command: `/usr/bin/gh gh api -H Accept: application/vnd.github+json /repos/Renumics/spotlight/dependabot/alerts?state=open&per_page=100` (http block)
>
> If you need me to access, download, or install something from one of these locations, you can either:
>
> - Configure [Actions setup steps](https://gh.io/copilot/actions-setup-steps) to set up my environment, which run before the firewall is enabled
> - Add the appropriate URLs or hosts to the custom allowlist in this repository's [Copilot coding agent settings](https://github.com/Renumics/spotlight/settings/copilot/coding_agent) (admins only)
>
> </details>
pr.author: Copilot
